tldr: Move CI/CD from CircleCI's general-purpose YAML pipelines to Codemagic, which is specialized for Flutter and mobile builds. The migration is mostly worthwhile when your workload is mobile-first; backend-only pipelines fit Codemagic less naturally.
why_switch: Purpose-built for Flutter, iOS, and Android
Built-in code signing and Fastlane support
Free tier available on monthly subscription
Simpler mobile build configuration than generic CI
what_to_export: Orbs and reusable commands in use
export_method: Pipeline source already lives in your repo at .circleci/config.yml; copy it as the migration starting point. Use the CircleCI API or web UI to list contexts and project-level environment variables so you can recreate them. Secret values cannot be read back from CircleCI; pull them from your password manager. Export iOS certificates and provisioning profiles from Apple Developer or your existing keychain export. Export Android keystores from secure storage. Download recent build artifacts only if you need them for historical reference. Document each orb you depend on, because Codemagic does not consume CircleCI orbs and you will need an equivalent script or built-in step.
import_method: In Codemagic, connect the same Git repository and create a new application. Configure builds via the Codemagic UI for the simplest setup, or commit a codemagic.yaml to your repo for config-as-code. Recreate environment variables and secrets in the Codemagic environment groups, marking signing material as secure. Upload iOS certificates and Android keystores into Codemagic's code signing section. Translate each CircleCI job into a Codemagic workflow with scripts blocks. Replace orbs with explicit script steps or built-in Codemagic integrations (Fastlane, Firebase, App Store Connect). Run a test build, compare artifacts and timings against CircleCI, then redirect default branches once green.
effort: 1-2 weeks for a mobile-only project, more if you also have non-mobile pipelines that need a different home.
gotchas: Codemagic is mobile-first; backend or web-only workloads fit awkwardly
CircleCI orbs have no direct equivalent; rewrite as scripts
Build minute economics differ; estimate before committing
Custom Docker executors used in CircleCI may not map directly
Parallel test splitting features differ from CircleCI's built-ins
Recheck signing flow on real devices before retiring CircleCI

email.subject: Switching CI to Codemagic, please cancel our CircleCI plan
email.body:
Hi CircleCI team,

Our engineering work has shifted heavily to mobile, so we are moving our CI/CD to Codemagic, which fits our Flutter and native mobile builds more naturally. After several productive years on CircleCI we want to wind things down cleanly.

Migration timeline is roughly 14 days. We will set up Codemagic, dual-run for about a week, then redirect main branches and stop new builds on CircleCI. We would like to keep the account in a read-only state for one more billing cycle so we can pull historical logs if needed, then close it.

Could you confirm:
- The cancellation effective date once we request closure
- Whether build logs remain accessible during a paused or downgraded plan
- Any export endpoints we should hit before closing

Thanks for the reliability over the years. This is a fit decision, not a quality complaint.

Your customer
